channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate as the ongoing war rattles global energy markets
item.description: New U.S. strikes on Iran and continued Iranian attacks on U.S. bases in the Gulf and Jordan are sending ripples through global energy markets.
item.content:encoded: New U.S. strikes on Iran and continued Iranian attacks on U.S. bases in the Gulf and Jordan are sending ripples through global energy markets.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: How the Houthis blockade of the Red Sea could affect the global economy
item.description: NPR's A Martínez asks former U.S. deputy national security adviser Jon Finer what the blockade of the Red Sea by Houthi rebels means for the global economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez asks former U.S. deputy national security adviser Jon Finer what the blockade of the Red Sea by Houthi rebels means for the global economy.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: FDA panel backs easier access to peptides
item.description: Advisers to the Food and Drug Administration are backing popular peptide therapies. They voted to allow specialized pharmacies to make them despite agency scientists' recommendations against it.
item.content:encoded: Advisers to the Food and Drug Administration are backing popular peptide therapies. They voted to allow specialized pharmacies to make them despite agency scientists' recommendations against it.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.
item.content:encoded: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Dalia Stasevska shines a light on Ukraine's neglected composers on new album
item.description: Dalia Stasevska is a rising star in the conducting world. On her new album, "Ukrainian Mixtape," she's promoting some of Ukraine's neglected composers.
item.content:encoded: Dalia Stasevska is a rising star in the conducting world. On her new album, "Ukrainian Mixtape," she's promoting some of Ukraine's neglected composers.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Military families share the challenges of deployment
item.description: With news of more U.S. casualties in the war with Iran, military families face the challenges of lengthy deployments.
item.content:encoded: With news of more U.S. casualties in the war with Iran, military families face the challenges of lengthy deployments.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: What families of U.S. service members are feeling as the Iran war continues
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Besa Pinchotti of the National Military Family Association what she's hearing from families of U.S. service members deployed overseas.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks Besa Pinchotti of the National Military Family Association what she's hearing from families of U.S. service members deployed overseas.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 15 years later, is the FDA's produce safety overhaul working?
item.description: Fifteen years ago, the FDA overhauled its safety rules for fruits and vegetables, hoping to prevent outbreaks of foodborne illness. How's that system working today?
item.content:encoded: Fifteen years ago, the FDA overhauled its safety rules for fruits and vegetables, hoping to prevent outbreaks of foodborne illness. How's that system working today?
item.dc:creator: Jeff Guo

item.title: New York Times lawyer talks about DOJ withdrawing subpoenas sent to Times' reporters
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to the New York Times' lead newsroom lawyer David McCraw about the Justice Department withdrawing subpoenas it had sent to the Times' reporters.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to the New York Times' lead newsroom lawyer David McCraw about the Justice Department withdrawing subpoenas it had sent to the Times' reporters.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump administration imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
item.description: The Trump administration has replaced expiring tariffs with new ones as it pursues its aggressive trade agenda.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has replaced expiring tariffs with new ones as it pursues its aggressive trade agenda.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
